message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -190,7 +190,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.7.4","title":"[RGC-604] Implement security-proof-guided specialization path for high-impact workloads","description":"Use security/authority proofs to unlock stronger specialization while preserving safety invariants and receipt-backed traceability.","design":"Build proof-guided specialization pipeline that attaches security/authority proof obligations to candidate hot paths, synthesizes specialization guards, and enforces runtime proof-epoch validity checks. Support deterministic deopt/rollback paths with receipt-backed evidence so specialized and unspecialized execution can be compared and recovered safely.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least one high-impact workload family runs through proof-guided specialization with measurable benefit and preserved semantics.
2. Unit tests validate guard soundness, proof-epoch invalidation behavior, and deterministic deopt/rollback transitions.
3. Integration/e2e equivalence suites prove specialized vs unspecialized behavioral parity under policy/proof changes.
4. Specialization receipts, rollback hooks, and replay artifacts are emitted for every specialized execution decision.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Future-self note: this is a differentiator track; keep it rigorous to avoid hype debt.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:21.343922887Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:08:36.414237637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","rgc","security","specialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.093462410Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:21.343922887Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:34.886560515Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2pv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:41:19.951075855Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.7.5","title":"[RGC-605] Optimize GC and allocator strategy for tail-latency stability","description":"Tune memory management for low pause variance and predictable throughput under extension-heavy workloads.","design":"Optimize GC/allocator behavior with workload-class-aware telemetry loops: allocation-site profiling, pause-budget enforcement, heap-growth policy tuning, and fragmentation controls. Couple tuning decisions to deterministic pause-distribution artifacts so tail-latency improvements are measurable and regression-resistant.","acceptance_criteria":"1. GC/allocator tuning demonstrates deterministic p95/p99 pause and throughput improvements on representative workload classes.
2. Unit tests cover allocator policy transitions, pause-budget threshold behavior, and memory-accounting invariants.
3. Integration/e2e stress runs emit run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt, and pause_distribution_report.json.
4. Tail-latency regression gates block unresolved critical degradations and preserve safety/invariant checks during tuning.
5. Artifacts include operator-readable guidance for memory-pressure remediation and configuration fallback.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Future-self note: p95/p99 behavior determines operator trust more than peak throughput.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticFox","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:21.736015506Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:21:21.644376435Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gc","performance","rgc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:21.736015506Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.299754639Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":659,"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.7.5","author":"RusticFox","text":"Kickoff (2026-03-03): claimed lane + reserved GC/allocator paths; baseline rch gate check passed. Evidence: artifacts/tail_latency_memory_hardening_regression_gate/20260303T231506Z/{run_manifest.json,events.jsonl}. Next: implement deterministic pause-distribution report + policy-transition tests in gc/gc_pause/alloc_domain scope.","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:21:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.8","title":"[RGC-700] Benchmarking, Profiling, and Regression Governance","description":"Track-level execution epic under the Reality Gap Closure program. This epic owns one coherent workstream with concrete delivery artifacts and explicit dependency boundaries.","design":"Creates credible benchmark methodology and regression governance with statistical rigor and reproducibility contracts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All child tasks close with deterministic tests and artifact evidence.
2. Dependency ordering is preserved; no child closes on bypassed prerequisites.
3. Structured logs and replay instructions are available for critical transitions.
4. rch-backed execution is used for CPU-intensive Rust build/test/bench flows.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"No artifact, no claim. No significance, no promotion.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:08.482284282Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-02-27T23:15:38.624654200Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","rgc","track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:08.482284282Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","title":"[RGC-701] Build apples-to-apples benchmark suite vs Node/Bun with pinned environments","description":"Create benchmark corpus and harness that compare equivalent workloads fairly across runtimes with pinned versions/configurations and environment fingerprints.","design":"Implement benchmark harness with strict fairness controls: pinned runtime versions/configuration, controlled warm-up policy, deterministic workload inputs, and environment fingerprint capture. Include both microbench and extension-heavy macro workloads so performance claims map to real operator experience, not synthetic wins.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests validate harness determinism, configuration pin enforcement, and fairness guardrails (warm-up/sample-count/timeout policy).
2. Integration/e2e benchmark executions emit run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt, plus benchmark_env_manifest.json and raw result archives.
3. Methodology documentation and machine-readable metadata make independent replay and cross-team verification straightforward.
4. Benchmark suite includes representative macro workloads that reflect user-facing extension/runtime behavior, not only microbench kernels.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Future-self note: credibility is lost permanently if benchmark methodology is sloppy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.142136791Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:11:53.923948295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","methodology","rgc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.731742919Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.523612855Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.142136791Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","title":"[RGC-701] Build apples-to-apples benchmark suite vs Node/Bun with pinned environments","description":"Create benchmark corpus and harness that compare equivalent workloads fairly across runtimes with pinned versions/configurations and environment fingerprints.","design":"Implement benchmark harness with strict fairness controls: pinned runtime versions/configuration, controlled warm-up policy, deterministic workload inputs, and environment fingerprint capture. Include both microbench and extension-heavy macro workloads so performance claims map to real operator experience, not synthetic wins.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests validate harness determinism, configuration pin enforcement, and fairness guardrails (warm-up/sample-count/timeout policy).
2. Integration/e2e benchmark executions emit run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt, plus benchmark_env_manifest.json and raw result archives.
3. Methodology documentation and machine-readable metadata make independent replay and cross-team verification straightforward.
4. Benchmark suite includes representative macro workloads that reflect user-facing extension/runtime behavior, not only microbench kernels.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Future-self note: credibility is lost permanently if benchmark methodology is sloppy.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaOwl","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.142136791Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:22:54.381201776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","methodology","rgc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.731742919Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.523612855Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.142136791Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.8.2","title":"[RGC-702] Implement statistical validation pipeline (variance controls, significance, confidence intervals)","description":"Apply rigorous stats to benchmark runs (warm-up policy, CV thresholds, confidence intervals, outlier policy, significance tests).","design":"Build deterministic statistical validation pipeline over benchmark outputs: variance controls, effect-size estimation, confidence intervals, outlier policy, and significance testing tuned for repeated CI execution. Emit machine-readable verdict objects that downstream gates can enforce without ambiguity.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests validate statistical computations (variance controls, confidence intervals, significance/effect-size calculations, outlier handling) with deterministic fixtures.
2. Integration tests verify pipeline behavior under low-noise, high-noise, and mixed-signal benchmark datasets.
3. E2E benchmark-validation runs emit run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt, and stats_verdict_report.json with reproducible decisions.
4. High-variance/low-confidence runs are automatically flagged/quarantined and cannot be promoted silently.
5. Performance claim gates require declared confidence/effect thresholds and fail closed when thresholds are unmet.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Future-self note: this bead prevents self-deception from noisy benchmark data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.554831767Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:11:54.143651866Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","rgc","statistics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.554831767Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:35.952932362Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.8.3","title":"[RGC-703] Add performance regression gate with automatic culprit isolation","description":"Continuously detect regressions and attribute likely culprit changesets/workloads to accelerate remediation.","design":"Implement a deterministic regression-gate engine that is CI-runner agnostic: baseline-window management, benchmark noise envelopes, culprit ranking across commit/workload deltas, and machine-readable verdict output (`regression_report.json`). Keep gate computation reproducible under pinned environment fingerprints so teams can replay exactly why a release was blocked.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Regression detector produces deterministic verdicts under fixed seeds, pinned benchmark inputs, and stable environment manifests.
2. Unit tests validate threshold math, variance/noise handling, and culprit-ranking behavior for synthetic regression patterns.
3. Integration/e2e benchmark runs emit run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt, and regression_report.json with reproducible evidence links.
4. Gate fails closed for unresolved critical regressions and supports time-bounded waiver records with owner + expiry metadata.
5. CI gate wiring is validated through RGC-055 integration without altering deterministic gate semantics.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"Ordering optimized: regression gate implementation no longer waits on generic CI wiring; instead CI wiring (RGC-055) now depends on this bead so the capability exists before global gate orchestration.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.967206537Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:07:26.105467391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","regression","rgc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:22.967206537Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:36.172813687Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsy.9","title":"[RGC-800] Conformance, Differential Verification, and Determinism Gates","description":"Track-level execution epic under the Reality Gap Closure program. This epic owns one coherent workstream with concrete delivery artifacts and explicit dependency boundaries.","design":"Enforces correctness gates: real test262 execution, lockstep differential oracle, fuzz/metamorphic campaigns, cross-arch replay determinism.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All child tasks close with deterministic tests and artifact evidence.
2. Dependency ordering is preserved; no child closes on bypassed prerequisites.
3. Structured logs and replay instructions are available for critical transitions.
4. rch-backed execution is used for CPU-intensive Rust build/test/bench flows.
Comprehensive verification obligation: completion requires focused unit tests, at least one integration/e2e scenario for user-facing behavior changes, and detailed structured logs/artifacts (run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, commands.txt) sufficient for deterministic replay and fast triage.","notes":"CoralStone claiming RGC-800 track ownership slice; starting with dependency-safe cross-arch reproducibility and replay-contract hardening work.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"CoralStone","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:08.934130869Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:04:29.158959193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["rgc","track","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:08.934130869Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsy.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:40:28.196850609Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
